raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Illustrating the power of a doctor's verbal framing to trigger autosuggestion, with contrasting outcomes for patient prognosis",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "A doctor's professional title alone confers suggestive influence on patients",
    "Negative pronouncements of incurability can initiate harmful autosuggestions",
    "Cautiously optimistic statements can produce unexpectedly favorable results through autosuggestion"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent; the statement maintains a clear logical contrast between two scenarios and their causal mechanisms without internal contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The statement is useful when the aim is to highlight psychological or placebo/nocebo dimensions of clinical communication and when its claims align with empirical evidence on suggestion effects",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statement becomes misleading if presented as established fact without evidence, if it exaggerates suggestion as a substitute for biomedical treatment, or if applied to cases where disease progression is independent of patient expectancy"
}
